steps.title: Convert percentage changes to multipliers
steps.content: When a value increases by 178%, we multiply by $1 + 1.78 = 2.78$. When it decreases by 21%, we multiply by $1 - 0.21 = 0.79$.

steps.title: Calculate the net percentage increase
steps.content: Apply both multipliers in sequence: $$2.78 \times 0.79 = 2.1962$$ This means the final value is \$2.1962$ times the initial value. Converting to a percentage increase: $$(2.1962 - 1) \times 100\% = 119.62\%$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
